name: Tensor-to-scalar ratio r from primordial gravitational waves
requirement: Any candidate framework with an inflationary or alternative early-universe phase must predict a tensor-to-scalar ratio r (amplitude of primordial GW power spectrum relative to scalar) below the BICEP/Keck 2021 upper bound r < 0.036 at 95% CL (at pivot scale k = 0.05 Mpc^-1).

regime.description: Inflationary horizon scales, ~10^16 GeV energy density if r ~ 0.01.

current_bound.reference: BICEP/Keck Collaboration, 'Improved Constraints on Primordial Gravitational Waves using Planck, WMAP, and BICEP/Keck Observations through the 2018 Observing Season', PRL 127, 151301 (2021), arXiv:2110.00483

what_it_excludes: r < 0.036 excludes large classes of large-field inflationary models, including phi^p chaotic inflation for p >= 2 (Linde) and natural inflation for f < M_Planck. Surviving inflationary models include R^2 (Starobinsky), alpha-attractors, hilltop, and small-field models. Future LiteBIRD, CMB-S4, Simons Observatory aim at sigma(r) ~ 10^-3.

parameterization.name: Standard inflationary parameterization: r, n_s (scalar tilt), n_t (tensor tilt), running. Single-field consistency relation: r = -8 n_t.
parameterization.reference: Baumann, 'TASI Lectures on Inflation', arXiv:0907.5424; Planck 2018 X (inflation): A&A 641, A10 (2020).
parameterization.summary: Inflation with potential V(phi) predicts r ~ 16 epsilon_V where epsilon_V is the slow-roll parameter; energy scale of inflation V^(1/4) = 1.04 x 10^16 GeV (r/0.01)^(1/4).

procedural.predicted_observable: B-mode polarization power spectrum of CMB at degree scales (l ~ 80), proportional to r.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: r < 0.036 at k = 0.05 Mpc^-1; with implied bound on inflationary potential height V < (1.6 x 10^16 GeV)^4.
framework_inputs: If the framework includes inflation, compute r and n_s in slow-roll.
If the framework is non-inflationary (bouncing cosmology, ekpyrotic, string-gas), compute the tensor power spectrum from the alternative mechanism.

primary_references.citation: BICEP/Keck Collaboration, 'Improved Constraints on Primordial Gravitational Waves using Planck, WMAP, and BICEP/Keck Observations through the 2018 Observing Season', PRL 127, 151301 (2021)
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. X. Constraints on inflation', A&A 641, A10 (2020)
